volumeTitle: The Little Carthaginian. Pseudolus. The Rope
label: Pseudolus (Section 2.4.755 - Section 3.2.879b)

sections.label: Section 2.4.755
sections.original: ceterum quo quicque pacto faciat , ipsi dixero .
sections.translation: As for the rest , in what way he is to do each thing , I ’ ll instruct himself .

sections.label: Section 2.4.756
sections.translation: Why , therefore , do we stand here then ?

sections.label: Section 2.4.756b
sections.translation: Bring the fellow to me just now , dressed out with all his accoutrements , to Aeschinus , the banker ’ s . But make all haste .

sections.label: Section 2.4.758b
sections.translation: We ’ ll be there before you .

sections.label: Section 2.4.758c
sections.translation: Get you gone there quickly . ( CALIDORUS and CHARINUS go into SIMO ’ S house .) Whatever before was uncertain or doubtful in my mind ,

sections.label: Section 2.4.760
sections.original: nunc liquet , nunc defaecatumst cor mihi ; nunc perviumst :
sections.translation: is now clear-now fined to the dregs ; my heart has now an open path . All my legions will I lead forth under their standards with happy omen [*] with favorable auspices , and to my heart ’ s content . I have a certainty that I can rout my enemies . Now will I go to the Forum , and load Simmia with my instructions

sections.label: Section 2.4.765
sections.original: quid agat , ne quid titubet , docte ut hanc ferat fallaciam .
sections.translation: what he is to do , that he may not be tripping at all , and that he may cleverly lay the train for this plot . Soon now shall I cause the town of this procurer to be carried by storm .

sections.label: Section 3.1.767
sections.translation: On that Boy on whom the Gods bestow servitude under a procurer , when they add a base occupation as well , assuredly do they , so far as I now understand in my mind ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.770
sections.original: malam rem magnam multasque aerumnas danunt .
sections.translation: bestow upon him a great misfortune and miseries manifold . Just as this servitude has turned out to me , where I am set over duties great and small ; nor am I able to find any admirer to love me , so that at length I might be fitted out in a little better guise .

sections.label: Section 3.1.775
sections.original: nunc huic lenoni hodie est natalis dies :
sections.translation: Now this day is the birthday of this procurer . The procurer has made a determination , from the lowest to the highest , that if each one does not this day send him a present , he shall perish to-morrow with the greatest torments . Now , faith , I know not what to do in my line ,

sections.label: Section 3.1.780
sections.original: neque ego illud possum , quod illi qui possunt solent .
sections.translation: for , unless I shall send a present to the procurer this day , to-morrow must I swallow down fullers ’ produce [*] . And yet I cannot do that which they who can are wont to do . Alas ! how little am I , even still for this vocation . And by my troth , now , to my misfortune how fearfully do I dread punishment .

sections.label: Section 3.1.785
sections.original: si quispiam det qui manus gravior siet ,
sections.translation: If any one lays on [*] whose hand is too heavy , although they say that it generally is done amid great weeping , I think that I am able in some measure to keep my teeth closed . But I must keep close my lips and my talking , for see , my master is betaking himself home , and bringing a Cook with him .

sections.label: Section 3.2.790
sections.original: Forum coquinum qui vocant , stulte vocant ,
sections.translation: Those who call it the cook ’ s market , call it so foolishly ; for ’ tis not a cook ’ s market , but a thieves ’ market . For if , upon oath , I were to seek out the worst of men , I couldn ’ t have brought a worse one than this fellow that I ’ m bringing , one , chattering , bragging , silly , and worthless .

sections.label: Section 3.2.795
sections.original: quin ob eam rem Orcus recipere ad se hunc noluit ,
sections.translation: Why , for this very reason Orcus has declined [*] to take him to himself , that he might be here to cook a banquet for the dead ; for here he is able to cook a thing to please them alone .

sections.label: Section 3.2.798
sections.original: Si me arbitrabare isto pacto , ut praedicas ,
sections.translation: If you thought of me in this manner that you are mentioning , why did you hire me ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.799b
sections.translation: From scarcity ; there wasn ’ t another .

sections.label: Section 3.2.800
sections.original: sed cur sedebas in foro , si eras coquos ,
sections.translation: But why , if you were a cook , were you sitting in the market-place , you alone behind the rest ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.801b
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you . By reason of the avarice of men have I become an inferior cook , not through my own inclination .

sections.label: Section 3.2.803b
sections.translation: For what reason is that ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.803c
sections.translation: I ’ ll tell you . Because , in fact , directly people come to hire a cook ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.805
sections.original: nemo illum quaerit qui optimus et carissimust :
sections.translation: no one enquires for him that ’ s the best and the highest priced : rather do they hire him that ’ s the lowest priced . Through this have I to-day been the only sitter in the market . Those wretched fellows are for a drachma a-piece ; not any person is able to prevail on me to rise for less than a didrachm [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.2.810
sections.original: non ego item cenam condio ut alii coqui ,
sections.translation: I don ’ t cook a dinner too , like other cooks , who bring me up seasoned meadows of grass upon their dishes ; who turn the guests into oxen , and supply the grass . This herbage , too , do they further season with other herbs : put in coriander , fennel , garlick , orage ;

sections.label: Section 3.2.815
sections.translation: they add , too , sorrel , cabbage , beet , and spinach . In this they dissolve a pound weight of asafoetida . The roguish mustard is pounded , which makes the eyes of those that pound it drop tears before they have pounded it . These fellows , when they cook dinners , when they do season them , season them ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.820
sections.translation: not with seasonings , but with vampyre owls [*] which eat out the bowels of the guests while still alive . Through this , in fact , it is , that people here live such short lives , inasmuch as they heap up these herbs of this sort in their stomachs , dreadful to be mentioned , not only to be eaten .

sections.label: Section 3.2.825
sections.original: quas herbas pecudes non edunt , homines edunt .
sections.translation: Herbage which the cattle eat not , men eat themselves .

sections.label: Section 3.2.826
sections.translation: What do you say ? Do you use divine seasonings , by which you can prolong the life of men , you , who find fault with these other seasonings ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.828b
sections.translation: I proclaim it boldly ;

sections.label: Section 3.2.829
sections.original: nam vel ducenos annos poterunt vivere
sections.translation: for those who shall eat of my victuals which I have seasoned will be able to exist two hundred years even .

sections.label: Section 3.2.831
sections.original: nam egó cocilendrum quando in patinas indidi
sections.translation: For when I ’ ve put into the saucepan either cicilendrum , or cepolindrum , or mace [*] , or saucaptis , the very dishes become warmed forthwith . These are sauces for fish , the cattle of Neptune ;

sections.label: Section 3.2.835
sections.translation: the flesh of the earthly cattle I season with cicimandrum , hapalopsis , or cataractria .

sections.label: Section 3.2.836b
sections.translation: Now may Jupiter and all the Divinities confound you with your sauces , and with all those lies of yours !

sections.label: Section 3.2.839
sections.translation: Do allow me to speak , please .

sections.label: Section 3.2.839b
sections.original: Loquere , atque i in malam crucem .
sections.translation: Speak , and go to very perdition .

sections.label: Section 3.2.840
sections.original: Vbi omnes patinae fervont , omnis aperio :
sections.translation: When all the saucepans are hot , I open them all then does the odour fly towards heaven with its hand hanging down [*] .

sections.label: Section 3.2.842
sections.translation: The odour with its hands hanging down ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.842b
sections.translation: I made a mistake without thinking .

sections.label: Section 3.2.843
sections.translation: How so ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.843b
sections.translation: With its feet hanging down , I meant to say . Jupiter dines on that odour every day .

sections.label: Section 3.2.845
sections.original: Si nusquam is coctum , quidnam cenat Iuppiter ?
sections.translation: If you happen not to go out to cook , pray what does Jupiter dine upon ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.846
sections.translation: He goes to sleep without his dinner .

sections.label: Section 3.2.846b
sections.translation: Go to very perdition . Is it for this reason that I ’ m to give you a didrachm to-day ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.848
sections.original: Fateór equidem esse me coquom carissumum ;
sections.translation: Well , I confess that I am a very high-priced cook ; but I make the results of my labour to be seen for the price ,

sections.label: Section 3.2.850
sections.translation: hired at which I go out .

sections.label: Section 3.2.850b
sections.translation: In thieving , to wit .

sections.label: Section 3.2.851
sections.original: An tu invenire postulas quemquam coquom
sections.translation: And do you expect to meet with any cook except with the claws of a kite or of an eagle ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.853
sections.original: An tu coquinatum te ire quoquam postulas ,
sections.translation: And do you expect to go anywhere to cook , and not to cook the dinner there with your claws tied up ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.855
sections.original: nunc adeo tu , qui meus es , iam edico tibi ,
sections.translation: Now , therefore , you boy ( to the BOY ) , who are my servant , I now give you notice to make haste to remove hence all my property ; and to keep his eyes as well in your sight . Whichever way he shall look , do you look the same way as well . If he shall move in any direction , do you move as well .

sections.label: Section 3.2.860
sections.original: manum sí protollet , pariter proferto manum :
sections.translation: If he shall put forth his hand , put you forth your hand as well . If he shall take anything of his own , do you suffer him to take it ; if he shall take what ’ s mine , do you on the other side hold him fast . If he shall stoop to the ground , do you stoop there as well .

sections.label: Section 3.2.865
sections.original: item his discipulis privos custodes dabo .
sections.translation: Likewise over your understrappers I shall appoint a single guard a-piece .

sections.label: Section 3.2.866
sections.translation: Only have good courage .

sections.label: Section 3.2.866b
sections.translation: Prithee , tell me how I possibly can have good courage , who am taking you home to my house ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.868
sections.original: Quia sorbitione faciam ego hodie te mea ,
sections.translation: Because , by my broth , this day will I do just in the way that Medea cooked up the old man Pelias [*]

sections.label: Section 3.2.870
sections.original: quem medicamento et suis venenis dicitur
sections.translation: whom she is said by a draught and by her potions from an aged man to have made young again ; so will I make you likewise .

sections.label: Section 3.2.872b
sections.translation: How now ; are you an enchanter as well ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.873
sections.translation: Why no , by my troth , I am rather a preserver [*] of mankind .

sections.label: Section 3.2.873b
sections.translation: Well now ; for how much would you teach me that one point in cooking ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.876
sections.translation: What point ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.876b
sections.original: Vt te servem , ne quid surripias mihi .
sections.translation: That I may preserve you from pilfering anything from me .

sections.label: Section 3.2.877
sections.original: Si credis , nummo ; si non , ne mina quidem .
sections.translation: For a didrachm , if you believe me ; if not , not for a mina even . But whether are you about to-day to give a dinner , to your friends or to your enemies ?

sections.label: Section 3.2.879b
sections.translation: Why , faith , to my friends surely .
